reasoning: As of October 2025, climate data for the first eight months of the year clearly demonstrates that 2025 is trailing the record-setting year of 2024. January through August ranked as the second-highest period on record. Furthermore, a La Niña event began in early 2025, exerting a cooling effect on annual global averages. Probabilistic forecasts from authoritative bodies, including NOAA and Carbon Brief, estimate the likelihood of 2025 being the warmest year on record at less than 1% as of Q3 2025. Given the substantial year-to-date deficit compared to 2024 and the prevailing La Niña conditions, a new record is practically impossible. I assign a minimal probability of 5%.
batch_queries: Will 2025 be the hottest year on record?
